schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Short, Practical Summary of the Differences (So You Can Act Now): V· S^(n)

theory.canonical_statement: ### Short, practical summary of the differences (so you can act now) - **Energy:** comoving observer sees extra energy from motion (kinetic + induced E-field). Eulerian observer sees frame-drag contributions from the metric (shift vector) that may not appear in the comoving frame. - **Momentum / Poynting:** directions and magnitudes differ by Lorentz/shift transforms. Regions where \(\mathbf v\cdot\mathbf S^{(n)}\) is large will show the biggest differences. - **Stresses / principal pressures:** eigenvalues shift under boost; magnetic tension along field lines persists but its projection onto principal axes moves with the frame. - **Off-diagonals:** generally larger for the Eulerian observer because of metric shift coupling; in a comoving orthonormal tetrad those can reduce but mechanical shear stays.
theory.technical_interpretation: Short, Practical Summary of the Differences (So You Can Act Now): V· S^(n) is a symbolic expression, written mathbf v·mathbf S^(n). It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from v, S, n. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
